name: gwtrigfind

metadata.name: gwtrigfind
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 or later (GPLv3+)
metadata.summary: Utility to find files archived by GW event trigger generators

documentation:
# GWTrigFind

A utility to find GW trigger files produced by event trigger generators and archived on the LIGO Data Grid.

[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/gwtrigfind.svg)](http://badge.fury.io/py/gwtrigfind)
[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/gwtrigfind/badge/?version=stable)](https://gwtrigfind.readthedocs.io/en/stable/?badge=stable)
[![Build status](https://git.ligo.org/detchar/tools/gwtrigfind/badges/main/pipeline.svg)](https://git.ligo.org/detchar/tools/gwtrigfind/-/pipelines)
![Code coverage](https://git.ligo.org/detchar/tools/gwtrigfind/badges/main/coverage.svg)

## Quickstart for python

To discover trigger files for a given channel:

```python
>>> from gwtrigfind import find_trigger_files
>>> cache = find_trigger_files(channel, etg, gpsstart, gpsend)
```

The resulting cache can then be read easily into a table using [`gwpy`](//gwpy.github.io/):

```python
>>> from gwpy.table import EventTable
>>> t = EventTable.read(cache, format='ligolw', tablename='sngl_burst')
```

The format argument depends on the etg you use, but is likely to be one of `'hdf5'`, `'ligolw'`, or `'root'`.

## Quickstart for command-line

The same query can be performed from the command line by passing each of the functional arguments above as arguments to the `gwtrigfind` executable script:

```bash
gwtrigfind <channel> <etg> <gpsstart> <gpsend>
```

modules.name: gwtrigfind
documentation:
Discover event trigger files in standard archive locations

`gwtrigfind` allows simple discovery of event trigger files generated by
automated processing of data from gravitational-wave detectors.

modules.name: gwtrigfind.core
documentation:
This module provides a discovery mechanism for LIGO_LW XML trigger
files written on the LIGO Data Grid according to the conventions in
LIGO-T1300468.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-
Copyright (C) Duncan Macleod (2014)
This file is part of GWTrigFind.
GWTrigFind is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify

functions.name: gwtrigfind.core.find_trigger_files
documentation:
Find the paths of trigger files for this channel and ETG.

This method uses an ETG-specific finder function to retrieve the
file paths.

# Parameters

channel

: name of data channel for which to search

etg

: name of trigger generator that processed the data

start

: GPS start time of search

end

: GPS end time of search

kwargs

: custom keyword arguments to pass down to the underlying finder

# Returns

files

: a list of file URLs

# See Also

gwtrigfind.find_detchar_files
gwtrigfind.find_daily_cbc_files
gwtrigfind.find_dmt_omega_files
gwtrigfind.find_kleinewelle_files
gwtrigfind.find_omega_online_files

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from gwtrigfind import find_trigger_files
>>> cache = find_trigger_files('L1:GDS-CALIB_STRAIN', 'Omicron', 1135641617, 1135728017)
```

functions.name: gwtrigfind.core.find_trigger_urls
documentation: DEPRECATED: use {func}`find_trigger_files` instead

functions.name: gwtrigfind.core.find_detchar_files
documentation:
Find files in the detchar home directory following T1300468

# Parameters

channel

: name of data channel for which to search

start

: GPS start time of search

end

: GPS end time of search

etg

: name of trigger generator that processed the data, defaults to
  `'omicron'`

ext

: file extension, defaults to `'h5'`

# Returns

files

: a list of file URLs

functions.name: gwtrigfind.core.find_kleinewelle_files
documentation:
Find KleineWelle output event files

# Parameters

channel

: name of data channel for which to search

start

: GPS start time of search

end

: GPS end time of search

base

: path of custom base directory, defaults to the LDG standard for
  the given `etg`

ext

: file extension, defaults to `'xml'`

# Returns

files

: a list of file URLs

functions.name: gwtrigfind.core.find_dmt_omega_files
documentation:
Find DMT-Omega trigger XML files.

# Parameters

channel

: name of data channel for which to search

start

: GPS start time of search

end

: GPS end time of search

base

: path of custom base directory, defaults to the LDG standard for
  the given `etg`

ext

: file extension, defaults to `'xml'`

# Returns

files

: a list of file URLs

functions.name: gwtrigfind.core.find_pycbc_live_files
documentation:
Find CBC pycbc live trigger files

Parameters
----------
channel : `str`
    name of data channel for which to search

start : `int`
    GPS start time of search

end : `int`
    GPS end time of search

base : `str`, optional
    the base path of the directory structure in which the event files
    are located, this should be the parent directory of the '%Y_%m_%d'
    directories

Returns
-------
files : `list` of `str`
    a list of file URLs

functions.name: gwtrigfind.core.find_daily_cbc_files
documentation:
Find daily CBC analysis trigger files

# Parameters

channel

: name of data channel for which to search

start

: GPS start time of search

end

: GPS end time of search

run

: name of daily CBC analysis that generated the files, defaults to
  `'bns_gds'`

filetag

: tag describing which kind of clustering was applied, defaults to
  `'30MILLISEC_CLUSTERED'`

ext

: file extension, defaults to `'xml.gz'`

# Returns

files

: a list of file URLs

functions.name: gwtrigfind.core.find_omega_online_files
documentation:
Find Omega triggers produced by online processes

This is only tested to work for the Omega online processing for GEO600

# Parameters

channel

: name of data channel for which to search

start

: GPS start time of search

end

: GPS end time of search

filetag

: tag describing which king of Omega files you want, defaults to
  `'DOWNSELECT'`

ext

: file extension, defaults to `'txt'`

# Returns

files

: a list of file URLs
